title: The Agent files a memo to Records Vault documenting the identified ~$6,250 Northstar Legal May 2026 IOLTA client-trust shortfall.
justification: The prompt explicitly asks the Agent to 'write it up as a memo to the file so there is a clear record of the problem and what it will take to make it right.' In this universe the engagement file is Records Vault. This rubric confirms the memo write action occurred AND that the filed memo documents the actual finding, not a generic review.
evidence: A memo is filed to Records Vault (write returns success) whose content documents the actual finding: a ~$6,250 May 2026 IOLTA trust shortfall traced to the Hartmann v. Sterling disbursement. Look in the Agent's filed document, not the environment state. Filing any Northstar IOLTA memo is NOT sufficient on its own. FAIL if the memo concludes the trust accounts 'tie to the penny' / balance, attributes the issue to undocumented draft or unposted JEs, or reports a different exposure figure (e.g., $161,674.13, $100,968.96, $62,761.76, $424,317.22) instead of the ~$6,250 Hartmann shortfall.

title: The Agent's memo states that the Northstar IOLTA client-trust cash is short of the client-trust liability (the amount owed to clients) by approximately $6,250 for the May 2026 period.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to 'establish whether the cash we are holding really matches the balances we are carrying for them.' Verified in the data: IOLTA trust cash (105000) is 2,460,578.36 and the IOLTA client-trust liability (250000) is 2,466,828.36 — a shortfall of $6,250.00.
evidence: In the Agent's memo content, a statement that trust cash is below the client-trust liability by about $6,250 (a number near $6,250 / $6,250.00 satisfies 'approximately'). FAIL if the memo reports the two balances as equal or as 'tying to the penny'; the pass requires stating trust cash (2,460,578.36) is BELOW the liability (2,466,828.36) by ~$6,250. A different exposure figure (e.g., $161,674.13, $100,968.96, $62,761.76) does not satisfy this rubric.

title: The Agent's memo identifies the Hartmann v. Sterling matter as the source of the trust difference.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to find 'on which matter' the issue arose. Verified: journal entry JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0061 (the trust disbursement causing the break) is on the Hartmann v. Sterling matter.
evidence: In the Agent's memo content, identification of the Hartmann v. Sterling matter as the source of the difference. The literal matter name 'Hartmann v. Sterling' (or the entry JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0061 / id je_a17f3c9e44b21d05) must appear. FAIL if the source is attributed instead to undocumented draft/unposted JEs, to Steven Perry or Ming Chang entries, or to unnamed/generic entries.

title: The Agent's memo states that the disbursement's offsetting entry was booked to operating cash (account 101000) instead of reducing the IOLTA client-trust liability (account 250000).
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to find 'where it ended up.' Verified: JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0061 credits trust cash (105000) $6,250 but debits operating cash (101000) rather than the client-trust liability (250000).
evidence: In the Agent's memo content, a statement that the disbursement's offset went to operating cash (101000) rather than the client-trust liability (250000). The memo must name at least one of the two account numbers explicitly (101000 or 250000) or the account titles 'Cash - Operating' / 'IOLTA Client Trust Liability'. FAIL if the memo describes the misposting only as 'cash to cash' or 'within cash accounts' without identifying that the offset hit firm operating cash rather than the client-trust liability.

title: The Agent's memo characterizes the difference as a regulatory client-trust (trust-rule / IOLTA) issue rather than a bank-timing item that will clear on its own, and explicitly rejects the preparer's 'in-transit / uncleared wire' explanation.
justification: The prompt directs: 'If trust funds have been pulled out of where they belong, say so plainly, because that is not a close-housekeeping item, it is a regulatory one.' The reconciliation's preparer note blames an uncleared wire; the memo must reject that framing AND attach the regulatory framing to the correct root cause.
evidence: In the Agent's memo content, BOTH (i) language framing the difference as a compliance / trust-rule / IOLTA / bar-regulation matter, AND (ii) explicit rejection of the in-transit / uncleared-wire / timing-item framing carried in the recon's variance explanation — e.g., 'this is not an in-transit wire,' 'the explanation does not hold,' 'the funds did not move to a trust bank.' FAIL if the memo only characterizes the issue as regulatory without rejecting the wire framing, or only rejects the wire framing without saying the matter is regulatory.

title: The Agent's memo states the corrective action needed: a journal entry that moves approximately $6,250 off operating cash (account 101000) and restores the trust position, explicitly naming the GL accounts involved.
justification: The prompt asks the memo to record 'what it will take to make it right.' Restoring the three-way trust tie requires removing the $6,250 from operating cash (101000) and either (a) putting it back into IOLTA trust cash (105000) by reversing JE-0061 or (b) reducing the client-trust liability (250000) to reflect the disbursement as a real client outflow. Verifier observation: a prior run passed on a vague 'cash to cash' description that never named the liability account; the rubric now requires the Agent to name the specific GL accounts so the corrective entry is unambiguous.
evidence: In the Agent's memo content, a stated correction of approximately $6,250 that (i) explicitly removes/credits operating cash account 101000 AND (ii) explicitly names the offsetting account as either IOLTA trust cash (105000) — i.e., a reversal of JE-0061 — or IOLTA client-trust liability (250000). The memo must name the actual account numbers or full account titles, not just 'restore the trust position' in narrative terms. FAIL if the memo describes the fix without naming 101000 as the account to be unwound, or without naming 105000 or 250000 as the offsetting account.

title: The Agent notifies matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com (the Northstar engagement partner) and elita.moore@brookfieldcpas.com (the reconciliation's preparer) of the finding.
justification: The prompt says to 'put it in front of Matthew along with whoever prepared the reconciliation.' Matthew Li is the Northstar Accounting Services Partner; the preparer of the IOLTA trust reconciliation is Elita Moore. The prompt names the goal (put it in front of them) but not a channel, so any outgoing notification (email or message) to both recipients satisfies it.
evidence: An outgoing communication from the Agent addressed to both matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com and elita.moore@brookfieldcpas.com (by email or message) that conveys the finding, returning success. Either recipient may be in the To or CC field — both routings count as 'notified.' The second recipient must be exactly elita.moore@brookfieldcpas.com. FAIL if the Agent notifies Matthew Li alone, or notifies Matthew Li plus a different contact (e.g., owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com, blue.evans@brookfieldcpas.com) in place of Elita Moore, even if other people are cc'd.

title: The Agent's notification to Matthew Li and Elita Moore states that the Northstar May IOLTA trust is short approximately $6,250.
justification: The notification must carry the substantive finding so the partner and preparer can act on it. The verified shortfall is $6,250 (105000 trust cash 2,460,578.36 vs 250000 client-trust liability 2,466,828.36).
evidence: In the Agent's outgoing message/email content, a statement of the ~$6,250 Northstar IOLTA trust shortfall. FAIL if the notification reports the trust accounts as equal / 'tying to the penny', states no shortfall amount, or communicates a different exposure figure (e.g., up to $161,674.13, $100,968.96, $62,761.76) instead of the ~$6,250 shortfall.

title: The Agent's notification to Matthew Li and Elita Moore attributes the shortfall to the Hartmann v. Sterling client-trust disbursement.
justification: So the partner and preparer can locate and correct it, the notification must name the source transaction. Verified: the shortfall traces to the Hartmann v. Sterling disbursement (JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0061).
evidence: In the Agent's outgoing message/email content, attribution of the shortfall to the Hartmann v. Sterling disbursement. The literal matter name 'Hartmann v. Sterling' (or the entry JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0061 / id je_a17f3c9e44b21d05) must appear. FAIL if the shortfall is attributed to undocumented draft/unposted JEs, to Steven Perry or Ming Chang entries, or to unnamed/generic entries.

title: The Agent's notification states that the correction must be made before the Q2 IOLTA client-trust attestation.
justification: The prompt frames the urgency as getting the correction made 'before this period moves any further and well before that attestation goes out.' The Q2 IOLTA client-trust attestation is the operative deadline.
evidence: In the Agent's outgoing message/email content, a reference to the Q2 IOLTA client-trust attestation deadline (or the need to fix it before that attestation) as the reason for urgency, tied to the correct finding being communicated (the ~$6,250 Hartmann v. Sterling trust shortfall). FAIL if the only stated basis for the Q2 hold is a different or unidentified issue (e.g., undocumented draft JEs, a missing three-way reconciliation, unposted entries) rather than the ~$6,250 shortfall.

title: The Agent independently verifies the IOLTA trust cash balance (account 105000) against the IOLTA client-trust liability balance (account 250000) for the May 2026 period rather than relying solely on the BlackLine reconciliation's reported balances.
justification: The prompt explicitly directs the Agent not to take the reconciliation's headline difference at face value ('do not take the headline difference at face value either') and to 'tie the trust position back to what we genuinely owe each client.' Independently confirming the GL position from a primary ledger source — separate from the BlackLine-reported figures — is required by every valid solution path. An Outcome rubric cannot capture this because the memo's stated balances could be mirrored from BlackLine's reconciliation_detail without any independent GL query, satisfying the content checks while skipping the verification the prompt demands.
evidence: In the Agent's trajectory, an independent retrieval of the May 2026 (FP-2026-05) balances for both account 105000 (IOLTA - Client Trust Account) and account 250000 (IOLTA Client Trust Liability) from a primary ledger source independent of the BlackLine reconciliation record. Listing the chart of accounts or pulling the journal entry alone does not satisfy this; the Agent must obtain the period-end balance for both accounts. FAIL if the Agent only reads the BlackLine reconciliation's gl_balance / supporting_balance fields and never independently queries the ledger for the two accounts.

title: The Agent posts a review note on the May Northstar IOLTA reconciliation (BL-7C2A9F4E1B83) that supersedes the preparer's 'uncleared wire' variance explanation by identifying the Hartmann v. Sterling trust shortfall as the actual cause of the variance.
justification: The prompt explicitly directs the Agent to 'leave a clear paper trail on the recon itself so anyone walking up to it sees the issue and stops.' A review note authored by the Agent on the reconciliation record itself is the canonical paper trail that supersedes the preparer's note. Without it, a subsequent reviewer walking up to BL-7C2A9F4E1B83 would still see Elita Moore's 'wire hasn't cleared' explanation as the only on-record account of the variance, and the recon could be quietly certified on a false framing.
evidence: In the Agent's trajectory, a successful review-note write on the reconciliation BL-7C2A9F4E1B83 whose content identifies the Hartmann v. Sterling matter and/or JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0061 as the actual source of the variance — not an in-transit wire. FAIL if no review note is posted on BL-7C2A9F4E1B83, if the note is posted on a different reconciliation, or if the note content fails to identify the Hartmann shortfall (for example, describes a generic 'pending investigation' or 'awaiting clarification' without naming the matter or the misposting).

title: The Agent creates an exception on the May Northstar IOLTA reconciliation (BL-7C2A9F4E1B83) documenting the approximately $6,250 IOLTA client-trust shortfall.
justification: The prompt directs the Agent to ensure the reconciliation 'cannot quietly get certified on the existing note.' A logged exception on the reconciliation is the structured mechanism that blocks certification — it remains visible to any subsequent reviewer attempting to submit, approve, or certify the recon. The exception must reflect the verified ~$6,250 shortfall and the trust / regulatory framing, not a generic placeholder.
evidence: In the Agent's trajectory, a successful exception-creation write tied to reconciliation BL-7C2A9F4E1B83 (via related_reconciliation_id or equivalent linkage), with the exception's description identifying the trust shortfall and referencing an amount at or near $6,250 OR naming the Hartmann v. Sterling matter / JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0061 as the root cause. FAIL if no exception is created against BL-7C2A9F4E1B83, if the exception is linked to a different reconciliation, or if the exception describes a different issue (e.g., a generic timing variance or unrelated control gap) without the trust-shortfall content.

title: The Agent attaches supporting evidence for the trust-shortfall finding to the May Northstar IOLTA reconciliation (BL-7C2A9F4E1B83).
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to leave a paper trail 'so anyone walking up to it sees the issue and stops.' The review note and exception flag the issue, but the attached evidence is what lets the next reviewer verify the finding without re-doing the entire investigation. Without an evidence attachment on the reconciliation record, the paper trail is incomplete — there is no source documentation tying the assertion of a shortfall back to the underlying ledger or JE detail.
evidence: In the Agent's trajectory, a successful evidence-attachment write on reconciliation BL-7C2A9F4E1B83 where the attached item is substantively related to the shortfall finding — for example, the JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0061 line detail showing DR 101000 / CR 105000 for $6,250, the GL balance proof for accounts 105000 and 250000 for FP-2026-05, the existing Records Vault support document doc_b94f2a17c8e34d6a for the Hartmann disbursement, or the Agent's own filed memo (per rubric #1) when that memo itself documents the JE, accounts, and balance gap. FAIL if no evidence is attached to BL-7C2A9F4E1B83, or if the attached item is unrelated to the shortfall (e.g., a generic close checklist, an unrelated reconciliation cover sheet, or a bare-title document with no shortfall content).
